gaps.snippet: use std :: fmt ;
gaps.reason: `use std::fmt` — `std` is not a sibling module OR sibling phylum transpiled in this same batch (an external crate, `std`, or simply out of this batch's target set). Flagged, not guessed (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An invalid UTF-8 byte sequence was found at a known byte index."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Produced by [`crate::from_utf8`] when the input bytes are not valid UTF-8."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C1 compliance"] # [doc = " The error is an explicit `Err(Utf8Error::Invalid { byte, reason })` — **never** a silent"] # [doc = " U+FFFD replacement-character substitution (RFC-0016 §4.1 C1; G2 \"never-silent\"). A caller"] # [doc = " who wants U+FFFD substitution must use a distinct, explicitly-named `*_lossy` variant"] # [doc = " (unavailable here — see spec §2 scope; `from_utf8` is the strict op)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C3 compliance"] # [doc = " The `byte` index names **where** the invalid byte is, so the caller can locate it in the"] # [doc = " input (the EXPLAIN artifact — RFC-0013 §4.1 I1 \"additive and still propagating\")."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum Utf8Error { # [doc = " The byte at index `byte` is not valid UTF-8."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `reason` is a short human-readable description of why the byte is invalid (C3 / G11)."] Invalid { # [doc = " The byte index at which the invalid byte was found."] byte : usize , # [doc = " A short human-readable reason (G11 dual projection; RFC-0013 §4.1)."] reason : & 'static str , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `Utf8Error` variant `Invalid` named field(s) `byte, reason` emitted positionally as `Invalid(Binary{64}, Bytes)` — Mycelium's `constructor` is positional-only (no record surface); product structure preserved, field names dropped

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An invalid UTF-8 byte sequence was found at a known byte index."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Produced by [`crate::from_utf8`] when the input bytes are not valid UTF-8."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C1 compliance"] # [doc = " The error is an explicit `Err(Utf8Error::Invalid { byte, reason })` — **never** a silent"] # [doc = " U+FFFD replacement-character substitution (RFC-0016 §4.1 C1; G2 \"never-silent\"). gaps.reason: enum `Utf8Error` derive(Clone) is a satisfied no-op under Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003 — every value already copies structurally; DN-128 §6.1) — not emitted as an impl, not a gap

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An invalid UTF-8 byte sequence was found at a known byte index."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Produced by [`crate::from_utf8`] when the input bytes are not valid UTF-8."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C1 compliance"] # [doc = " The error is an explicit `Err(Utf8Error::Invalid { byte, reason })` — **never** a silent"] # [doc = " U+FFFD replacement-character substitution (RFC-0016 §4.1 C1; G2 \"never-silent\"). gaps.snippet: impl fmt :: Display for Utf8Error { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> fmt :: Result { match self { Utf8Error :: Invalid { byte , reason } => { write ! (f , "invalid UTF-8 at byte {byte}: {reason}") } } } }
gaps.reason: trait-impl of non-prelude trait `Display` — no ambient trait definition and synthetic trait emission is refused (DN-34 §8.8 / DN-122 MVP); gapped the whole impl so the residual cannot file-poison myc-check (G2; express gap-close 2026-07-16)

gaps.snippet: mycelium_std_core :: impl_std_error ! (Utf8Error) ;
gaps.reason: item-position macro invocation — no macro system in this grammar

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " An index into a `Text` was out of range or fell on an invalid boundary."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Produced by [`crate::slice`] and [`crate::char_at`] when the requested index is not a valid"] # [doc = " char/grapheme boundary (or is out of range)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C1 compliance"] # [doc = " The error is an explicit `Err(BoundaryError::…)` — **never** a silent snap to the nearest"] # [doc = " boundary or a truncation (RFC-0016 §4.1 C1; G2)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C3 compliance"] # [doc = " Each variant carries the offending index and/or the valid length so the caller can diagnose"] # [doc = " the failure without reparsing (RFC-0013 §4.1 I1)."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum BoundaryError { # [doc = " The requested index or range end is past the end of the string."] OutOfRange { # [doc = " The length of the string in bytes."] len : usize , # [doc = " The index (or range end) that was requested."] asked : usize , } , # [doc = " The byte index falls in the middle of a multi-byte codepoint."] NotCharBoundary { # [doc = " The offending byte index."] byte : usize , } , # [doc = " The byte index falls in the middle of a grapheme cluster."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — Q2 (grapheme segmentation)"] # [doc = " This variant depends on a versioned Unicode grapheme-break table (spec §7-Q2)."] # [doc = " The table version is not yet reified in `Meta` (a maintainer call). The variant"] # [doc = " exists so the error type is complete; `slice` with grapheme-boundary checking"] # [doc = " will be gated until the table versioning is resolved (spec §7-Q2)."] NotGraphemeBoundary { # [doc = " The offending byte index."] byte : usize , } , # [doc = " The requested range is inverted (`start > end`)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Both endpoints can individually be in range yet describe an empty/negative span; reported"] # [doc = " explicitly rather than panicking on the slice index (C1 / G2 — never a runtime panic where"] # [doc = " the signature promises `Result`)."] InvalidRange { # [doc = " The requested start byte index."] start : usize , # [doc = " The requested end byte index (`< start`)."] end : usize , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `BoundaryError` variant `OutOfRange` named field(s) `len, asked` emitted positionally as `OutOfRange(Binary{64}, Binary{64})` — Mycelium's `constructor` is positional-only (no record surface); product structure preserved, field names dropped

gaps.snippet: impl fmt :: Display for BoundaryError { fn fmt (& self , f : & mut fmt :: Formatter < '_ >) -> fmt :: Result { match self { BoundaryError :: OutOfRange { len , asked } => { write ! (f , "index {asked} is out of range (string length {len})") } BoundaryError :: NotCharBoundary { byte } => { write ! (f , "byte index {byte} is not on a char boundary") } BoundaryError :: NotGraphemeBoundary { byte } => { write ! (f , "byte index {byte} is not on a grapheme-cluster boundary") } BoundaryError :: InvalidRange { start , end } => { write ! (f , "inverted range: start {start} is greater than end {end}") } } } }
gaps.reason: trait-impl of non-prelude trait `Display` — no ambient trait definition and synthetic trait emission is refused (DN-34 §8.8 / DN-122 MVP); gapped the whole impl so the residual cannot file-poison myc-check (G2; express gap-close 2026-07-16)

gaps.snippet: mycelium_std_core :: impl_std_error ! (BoundaryError) ;
gaps.reason: item-position macro invocation — no macro system in this grammar

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A parse failure: the input string was empty, malformed, or the lexically-valid value was"] # [doc = " out of range for the target type."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Produced by [`crate::parse_int`] and [`crate::parse_bool`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Honesty crux (C1 / RFC-0016 §4.4)"] # [doc = " `parse` returns a **`Result`, never a sentinel** (`0` / `false` / `\"\"`). A malformed input is"] # [doc = " `Err(ParseErr)` carrying the byte/char index + expected-vs-found (the RFC-0013 §4.1 diagnostic"] # [doc = " record I1). The caller cannot silently ignore the failure."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C3 compliance"] # [doc = " `Invalid { at, expected, found }` carries **where** the parse failed and **what** was expected"] # [doc = " vs found — the EXPLAIN artifact (RFC-0013 §4.1 I1 \"additive over the explicit error\")."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — Q3 (parse ↔ numerics value-semantics seam)"] # [doc = " `ParseErr::OutOfRange { at, target }` is the hand-off point: `text` owns the lexical failure,"] # [doc = " `math`/`numerics` own the value-range semantics. See module-level FLAG Q3."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum ParseErr { # [doc = " The input string was empty."] Empty , # [doc = " The input was lexically invalid at the given byte index."] Invalid { # [doc = " The byte index at which the unexpected character was found."] at : usize , # [doc = " A short description of what was expected at this position (G11)."] expected : & 'static str , # [doc = " A short description of what was actually found (G11)."] found : String , } , # [doc = " The digit sequence was lexically valid but outside the range of `target`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — Q3"] # [doc = " `text` reports the out-of-range condition; the numeric module (M-525/M-512) owns the"] # [doc = " authoritative range semantics. This is the `text`-layer hand-off only."] OutOfRange { # [doc = " The byte index at which the out-of-range value starts."] at : usize , # [doc = " The target type that the value was being parsed into."] target : & 'static str , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `ParseErr` variant `Invalid` named field(s) `at, expected, found` emitted positionally as `Invalid(Binary{64}, Bytes, Bytes)` — Mycelium's `constructor` is positional-only (no record surface); product structure preserved, field names dropped

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A parse failure: the input string was empty, malformed, or the lexically-valid value was"] # [doc = " out of range for the target type."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Produced by [`crate::parse_int`] and [`crate::parse_bool`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Honesty crux (C1 / RFC-0016 §4.4)"] # [doc = " `parse` returns a **`Result`, never a sentinel** (`0` / `false` / `\"\"`). A malformed input is"] # [doc = " `Err(ParseErr)` carrying the byte/char index + expected-vs-found (the RFC-0013 §4.1 diagnostic"] # [doc = " record I1). The caller cannot silently ignore the failure."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C3 compliance"] # [doc = " `Invalid { at, expected, found }` carries **where** the parse failed and **what** was expected"] # [doc = " vs found — the EXPLAIN artifact (RFC-0013 §4.1 I1 \"additive over the explicit error\")."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — Q3 (parse ↔ numerics value-semantics seam)"] # [doc = " `ParseErr::OutOfRange { at, target }` is the hand-off point: `text` owns the lexical failure,"] # [doc = " `math`/`numerics` own the value-range semantics. See module-level FLAG Q3."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum ParseErr { # [doc = " The input string was empty."] Empty , # [doc = " The input was lexically invalid at the given byte index."] Invalid { # [doc = " The byte index at which the unexpected character was found."] at : usize , # [doc = " A short description of what was expected at this position (G11)."] expected : & 'static str , # [doc = " A short description of what was actually found (G11)."] found : String , } , # [doc = " The digit sequence was lexically valid but outside the range of `target`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — Q3"] # [doc = " `text` reports the out-of-range condition; the numeric module (M-525/M-512) owns the"] # [doc = " authoritative range semantics. This is the `text`-layer hand-off only."] OutOfRange { # [doc = " The byte index at which the out-of-range value starts."] at : usize , # [doc = " The target type that the value was being parsed into."] target : & 'static str , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `ParseErr` variant `OutOfRange` named field(s) `at, target` emitted positionally as `OutOfRange(Binary{64}, Bytes)` — Mycelium's `constructor` is positional-only (no record surface); product structure preserved, field names dropped

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A parse failure: the input string was empty, malformed, or the lexically-valid value was"] # [doc = " out of range for the target type."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Produced by [`crate::parse_int`] and [`crate::parse_bool`]."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # Honesty crux (C1 / RFC-0016 §4.4)"] # [doc = " `parse` returns a **`Result`, never a sentinel** (`0` / `false` / `\"\"`). A malformed input is"] # [doc = " `Err(ParseErr)` carrying the byte/char index + expected-vs-found (the RFC-0013 §4.1 diagnostic"] # [doc = " record I1). The caller cannot silently ignore the failure."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # C3 compliance"] # [doc = " `Invalid { at, expected, found }` carries **where** the parse failed and **what** was expected"] # [doc = " vs found — the EXPLAIN artifact (RFC-0013 §4.1 I1 \"additive over the explicit error\")."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — Q3 (parse ↔ numerics value-semantics seam)"] # [doc = " `ParseErr::OutOfRange { at, target }` is the hand-off point: `text` owns the lexical failure,"] # [doc = " `math`/`numerics` own the value-range semantics. See module-level FLAG Q3."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq , Eq)] pub enum ParseErr { # [doc = " The input string was empty."] Empty , # [doc = " The input was lexically invalid at the given byte index."] Invalid { # [doc = " The byte index at which the unexpected character was found."] at : usize , # [doc = " A short description of what was expected at this position (G11)."] expected : & 'static str , # [doc = " A short description of what was actually found (G11)."] found : String , } , # [doc = " The digit sequence was lexically valid but outside the range of `target`."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " # FLAG — Q3"] # [doc = " `text` reports the out-of-range condition; the numeric module (M-525/M-512) owns the"] # [doc = " authoritative range semantics. This is the `text`-layer hand-off only."] OutOfRange { # [doc = " The byte index at which the out-of-range value starts."] at : usize , # [doc = " The target type that the value was being parsed into."] target : & 'static str , } , }
gaps.reason: enum `ParseErr` derive(Clone) is a satisfied no-op under Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003 — every value already copies structurally; DN-128 §6.1) — not emitted as an impl, not a gap

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " `Utf8Error::Invalid` carries the byte index and reason (C3 — EXPLAIN artifact)."] # [test] fn utf8_error_display_carries_index_and_reason () { let e = Utf8Error :: Invalid { byte : 3 , reason : "continuation byte expected" , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("3") , "must include byte index") ; assert ! (s . contains ("continuation byte expected") , "must include reason") ; } # [test] fn utf8_error_is_std_error () { let e = Utf8Error :: Invalid { byte : 0 , reason : "test" , } ; let _ : & dyn std :: error :: Error = & e ; } # [doc = " Out-of-range variant carries len and asked (C3)."] # [test] fn boundary_error_out_of_range_display () { let e = BoundaryError :: OutOfRange { len : 5 , asked : 10 } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("10") , "must include asked index") ; assert ! (s . contains ("5") , "must include length") ; } # [doc = " NotCharBoundary variant carries the byte index (C3)."] # [test] fn boundary_error_not_char_boundary_display () { let e = BoundaryError :: NotCharBoundary { byte : 2 } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("2") , "must include byte index") ; } # [doc = " NotGraphemeBoundary variant carries the byte index (C3)."] # [test] fn boundary_error_not_grapheme_boundary_display () { let e = BoundaryError :: NotGraphemeBoundary { byte : 4 } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("4") , "must include byte index") ; } # [test] fn boundary_error_is_std_error () { let e = BoundaryError :: OutOfRange { len : 1 , asked : 2 } ; let _ : & dyn std :: error :: Error = & e ; } # [doc = " Empty parse error has no sentinel in its display."] # [test] fn parse_err_empty_display () { let s = ParseErr :: Empty . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("empty") , "must mention empty") ; } # [doc = " Invalid variant carries the byte index, expected, and found (C3 — RFC-0013 §4.1 I1)."] # [test] fn parse_err_invalid_display_carries_where_and_what () { let e = ParseErr :: Invalid { at : 7 , expected : "digit" , found : "x" . to_string () , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("7") , "must include byte index") ; assert ! (s . contains ("digit") , "must include expected") ; assert ! (s . contains ("x") , "must include found") ; } # [doc = " OutOfRange variant carries the byte index and target type (C3 / FLAG Q3)."] # [test] fn parse_err_out_of_range_display_carries_at_and_target () { let e = ParseErr :: OutOfRange { at : 0 , target : "i64" , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("i64") , "must include target type") ; assert ! (s . contains ("0") , "must include byte index") ; } # [test] fn parse_err_is_std_error () { let e = ParseErr :: Empty ; let _ : & dyn std :: error :: Error = & e ; } # [doc = " EncodeError carries the unrepresentable char, its index, and the target encoding (C3)."] # [test] fn encode_error_unrepresentable_display () { let e = EncodeError :: Unrepresentable { ch : '€' , at : 5 , target_encoding : "Latin-1" , } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ('€' . to_string () . as_str ()) , "must include the char") ; assert ! (s . contains ("5") , "must include char index") ; assert ! (s . contains ("Latin-1") , "must include target encoding") ; } # [test] fn encode_error_is_std_error () { let e = EncodeError :: Unrepresentable { ch : 'X' , at : 0 , target_encoding : "Latin-1" , } ; let _ : & dyn std :: error :: Error = & e ; } # [doc = " UnpairedSurrogate carries the unit index (C3)."] # [test] fn transcode_error_unpaired_surrogate_display () { let e = TranscodeError :: UnpairedSurrogate { at : 3 } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("3") , "must include unit index") ; assert ! (s . contains ("surrogate") , "must mention surrogate") ; } # [doc = " Invalid carries the unit index (C3)."] # [test] fn transcode_error_invalid_display () { let e = TranscodeError :: Invalid { at : 8 } ; let s = e . to_string () ; assert ! (s . contains ("8") , "must include unit index") ; } # [test] fn transcode_error_is_std_error () { let e = TranscodeError :: UnpairedSurrogate { at : 0 } ; let _ : & dyn std :: error :: Error = & e ; } # [doc = " Different `BoundaryError` variants produce distinct display strings (G11 dual projection)."] # [doc = " Guard: returning the same string for every variant makes this fail."] # [test] fn boundary_error_variants_have_distinct_displays () { let oor = BoundaryError :: OutOfRange { len : 10 , asked : 20 } . to_string () ; let ncb = BoundaryError :: NotCharBoundary { byte : 1 } . to_string () ; let ngb = BoundaryError :: NotGraphemeBoundary { byte : 1 } . to_string () ; assert_ne ! (oor , ncb) ; assert_ne ! (oor , ngb) ; assert_ne ! (ncb , ngb) ; } # [doc = " Different `ParseErr` variants produce distinct display strings (G11)."] # [doc = " Guard: returning the same string for every variant makes this fail."] # [test] fn parse_err_variants_have_distinct_displays () { let empty = ParseErr :: Empty . to_string () ; let invalid = ParseErr :: Invalid { at : 0 , expected : "digit" , found : "a" . to_string () , } . to_string () ; let oor = ParseErr :: OutOfRange { at : 0 , target : "i32" , } . to_string () ; assert_ne ! (empty , invalid) ; assert_ne ! (empty , oor) ; assert_ne ! (invalid , oor) ; } }
